title: Ceratocephalus
intro: Ceratocephalus, also known as Mouse Head or False Harebell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Ceratocephalus is commonly used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to alleviate respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
taxonomy: Ceratocephalus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Ranunculaceae. This family is commonly known as the buttercup family. The Ranunculaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes over 2,000 species. Ceratocephalus species are typically found in the Northern Hemisphere, particularly in temperate and alpine regions. They are known for their distinctive, often yellow or white flowers.
